commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer proof handling, not a narrow code repair for automation.; Cleared: Latest head canonicalizes the raw stale path before containment and adds symlink escape coverage, so I did not find a remaining concrete security-boundary defect in the diff. (sha=57898de9b6a8f1744e4801c0a93f37141302c7e2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a canonical legacy-root-only raw stale session transcript candidate and tests for legacy-root inclusion, outside-root exclusion, and symlink escape exclusion.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +73. Total +105 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main can synthesize a current-session transcript candidate instead of preserving a raw legacy-root stale path when resolveSessionFilePath rejects a stale location outside the agent sessions tree.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer proof handling, not a narrow code repair for automation.

target.title: #91868: Include raw stale path in transcript candidate resolution
target.body:
"## Summary

Constrain `resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates()` to only include a raw stale `sessionFile` path when it resolves under a known OpenClaw-owned legacy transcript root (`~/.openclaw/sessions/`). Without this fix, legacy `.jsonl` transcript files at `~/.openclaw/sessions/` (pre-per-agent migration) are silently dropped from the candidate list when `resolveSessionFilePath()` throws for paths outside the agent sessions tree, causing them to persist on disk after session reset.

The constraint prevents archive/reset operations from renaming arbitrary filesystem paths while still catching legacy transcript files that predate the per-agent filing overhaul.

## Root Cause

When a session entry's `sessionFile` references a path whose basename UUID doesn't match the current `sessionId` (classified as "stale"), `resolveSessionFilePath()` is called but may throw when the path lies outside the agent sessions directory tree (e.g. a legacy `~/.openclaw/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl` path from before the per-agent filing overhaul). The `pushCandidate()` wrapper silently catches the throw, dropping the stale path from the candidate list entirely. The legacy fallback at the end of the function generates a path from `sessionId`, not from the original stale path, so it can't compensate.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Include the raw resolved `sessionFile` path as an archive candidate only when it resolves under a known OpenClaw-owned legacy transcript root (`~/.openclaw/sessions/`). The stale path is constrained by checking `path.relative(legacySessionsDir, resolved)` — only paths that resolve under the legacy root are added. This prevents session reset/delete operations from mutating files outside OpenClaw transcript directories.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112, Node.js via tsx, openclaw workspace importing the production `resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates` module from `src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.ts`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
1. Create a stale .jsonl transcript under a simulated .openclaw/sessions/ (legacy root)
2. Create a stale .jsonl transcript outside any known legacy root (/tmp/outside/)
3. Call resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates() with storePath and the legacy stale path
4. Verify the legacy stale path IS included in candidates
5. Call resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates() with storePath and the outside stale path
6. Verify the outside path is NOT included
7. Repeat with agentId (no storePath) for the legacy path — verify included
```

Evidence after fix:
```
═══ Scenario 1: stale path UNDER legacy root (should be included) ═══
  stale path: /tmp/oc-repro-X8Wsvm/.openclaw/sessions/11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.jsonl
  included in candidates: true

═══ Scenario 2: stale path OUTSIDE legacy root (should be excluded) ═══
  stale path: /tmp/oc-repro-X8Wsvm/outside/11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.jsonl
  included in candidates: false

═══ Scenario 3: stale path under legacy root, no storePath + agentId ═══
  stale path: /tmp/oc-repro-X8Wsvm/.openclaw/sessions/11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.jsonl
  included in candidates: true

PASS
```

Observed result after fix: Legacy stale paths under `~/.openclaw/sessions/` are correctly included as archive candidates. Arbitrary outside-root stale paths are correctly excluded. All 336 tests pass (316 session-utils + 20 archive events), including the new regression tests for the legacy root constraint. The fix covers all three stale-path code branches with the same constraint helper.

What was not tested: No additional gaps identified. Change is minimal (+33/-7 across 2 files) — the constraint logic, stale-path branches, and regression tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] Regression tests pass: positive case (legacy root stale path included) + negative case (outside-root path excluded)
- [x] All 336 existing tests continue to pass
- [x] Change is minimal (+33/-7 across 2 files) and targeted
- [x] Reproducibility confirmed via terminal output from production module

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
"
target.author: pandah97

target.title: #91868: Include raw stale path in transcript candidate resolution
target.body:
"## Summary

```
═══ Scenario 1: stale path UNDER legacy root (should be included) ═══

═══ Scenario 2: stale path OUTSIDE legacy root (should be excluded) ═══

```

All 336 tests pass (316 session-utils + 20 archive events), including the new regression tests for the legacy root constraint. ## Regression Test Plan
